parsed_generation.narration: An eye-level sequence tracks a person walking across the Abbey Road zebra crossing from approach to mid-crossing. The shots evolve from a broad front view to a tight close-up and a side-angle, emphasizing motion through the crossing.

feedback:
The gold standard calls for a static eye-level shot of the Abbey Road crossing with perspective lines and stopped cars, followed by a shot of a person stepping onto the white stripes. The generated sequence uses a front view, varying angles, and mismatched timing that doesn’t emphasize the crossing from an eye-level perspective. To fix, adopt the two-shot structure and exact timing: [00:00-00:04] eye-level static crossing with cars, then [00:04-00:08] the person steps onto the stripes toward the center, using precise camera angles (eye-level wide/static first, then a mid-shot).
